title: gh#28: Tests: Shared storage backend coverage gaps
reasoning:
Imported from https://github.com/joshgk00/social-media-scheduler/issues/28.
Backlog section: Tests.

## Problem

Shared storage has several missing regression tests around stream handling, S3 error behavior, backend factory validation, and lifecycle cleanup.

The original issue also included `MediaDropZone` validation tests. That web/component work has been split into #119 so this issue can stay focused on `@sms/shared` storage behavior.

## Scope

Add focused tests in `packages/shared/src/__tests__/storage.test.ts` for the shared storage layer.

## Acceptance criteria

- `LocalStorage.save()` is tested with a `ReadableStream` or Node readable input path, not only `Buffer` input.
- `S3Storage.exists()` returns `false` for a thrown `NoSuchKey` error name.
- `S3Storage.exists()` rethrows non-404/non-missing errors.
- `S3Storage.get()` throws a clear error when `response.Body` is undefined.
- `createStorageBackend()` throws for an invalid `MEDIA_STORAGE_BACKEND` value.
- If #27 lands first, storage lifecycle tests cover:
  - `LocalStorage.destroy()` resolves without side effects.
  - `S3Storage.destroy()` calls the mocked client's `destroy()` method.
- `pnpm --filter @sms/shared test -- storage` passes, or the closest focused storage test command.

## Out of scope

- `MediaDropZone` validation tests; tracked in #119.
- Changing storage backend behavior beyond what #27 requires.
- Moving or lazy-loading the AWS SDK; tracked in #11.

## References

- Storage lifecycle/contentType decision: #27
- Optional AWS dependency cleanup: #11
- MediaDropZone validation test split: #119
reproduction:

The original issue also included `MediaDropZone` validation tests. That web/component work has been split into #119 so this issue can stay focused on `@sms/shared` storage behavior.
recommendation: Add focused tests in `packages/shared/src/__tests__/storage.test.ts` for the shared storage layer.
whyTestsDoNotAlreadyCoverThis: Imported from GitHub issue. Preserve or add focused regression coverage when the issue acceptance criteria calls for it.
